stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 3 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability of 78%. sensor 2 has a reliability between 78% and 88% (uncertain due to limited operational data). sensor 3 has a reliability of 85%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all sensors simultaneously, estimated between 2.2% and 6.6%. What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.9272 to 0.9741. Point estimate: 0.9506.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 sensors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.22 × 0.15 = 0.0073. P(works) = 0.9927.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.12 × 0.15 = 0.004. P(works) = 0.996.
With common-cause failure (2.2%-6.6%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9272, 0.9741].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
